# power

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `power`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `power.ups.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable support for Power Devices, such as Uninterruptible Power Supplies, Power Distribution Units and Solar Controllers . |
| `power.ups.maxStartDelay` | `signed integer` | This can be set as a global variable above your first UPS definition and it can also be set in a UPS section. This value controls how long upsdrvctl will wait for the driver to finish starting. This keeps your system from getting stuck due to a broken driver or UPS. |
| `power.ups.mode` | `one of "none", "standalone", "netserver", "netclient"` | The MODE determines which part of the NUT is to be started, and which configuration files must be modified. The values of MODE can be: - none: NUT is not configured, or use the Integrated Power Management, or use some external system to startup NUT components. So nothing is to be started. - standalone: This mode address a local only configuration, with 1 UPS protecting the local system. This implies to start the 3 NUT layers (driver, upsd and upsmon) and the matching configuration files. This mode can also address UPS redundancy. - netserver: same as for the standalone configuration, but also need some more ACLs and possibly a specific LISTEN directive in upsd.conf. Since this MODE is opened to the network, a special care should be applied to security concerns. - netclient: this mode only requires upsmon. |
| `power.ups.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Open ports in the firewall for `upsd`. |
| `power.ups.package` | `package` | The nut package to use. |
| `power.ups.schedulerRules` | `string` | File which contains the rules to handle UPS events. |
| `power.ups.ups` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | This is where you configure all the UPSes that this system will be monitoring directly. These are usually attached to serial ports, but USB devices are also supported. |
| `power.ups.ups.<name>.description` | `string` | Description of the UPS. |
| `power.ups.ups.<name>.directives` | `list of string` | List of configuration directives for this UPS. |
| `power.ups.ups.<name>.driver` | `string` | Specify the program to run to talk to this UPS. apcsmart, bestups, and sec are some examples. |
| `power.ups.ups.<name>.maxStartDelay` | `null or signed integer` | This can be set as a global variable above your first UPS definition and it can also be set in a UPS section. This value controls how long upsdrvctl will wait for the driver to finish starting. This keeps your system from getting stuck due to a broken driver or UPS. |
| `power.ups.ups.<name>.port` | `string` | The serial port to which your UPS is connected. /dev/ttyS0 is usually the first port on Linux boxes, for example. |
| `power.ups.ups.<name>.shutdownOrder` | `signed integer` | When you have multiple UPSes on your system, you usually need to turn them off in a certain order. upsdrvctl shuts down all the 0s, then the 1s, 2s, and so on. To exclude a UPS from the shutdown sequence, set this to -1. |
| `power.ups.ups.<name>.summary` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Lines which would be added inside ups.conf for handling this UPS. |
| `power.ups.upsd` | `submodule` | Options for the `upsd.conf` configuration file. |
| `power.ups.upsd.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable `upsd`. |
| `power.ups.upsd.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Additional lines to add to `upsd.conf`. |
| `power.ups.upsd.listen` | `list of (submodule)` | Address of the interface for `upsd` to listen on. See `man upsd` for details`. | | `power.ups.upsd.listen.*.address`|`string`| Address of the interface for`upsd`to listen on. See`man upsd.conf`for details. | |`power.ups.upsd.listen.*.port`|`16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)`| TCP port for`upsd`to listen on. See`man upsd.conf`for details. | |`power.ups.upsmon`|`submodule`| Options for the`upsmon.conf`configuration file. | |`power.ups.upsmon.enable`|`boolean`| Whether to enable`upsmon`. | | `power.ups.upsmon.group`|`string`| Group for the default`nutmon`user. If the default user is created and this is not specified, a default group will be created. | |`power.ups.upsmon.monitor`|`attribute set of (submodule)`| Set of UPS to monitor. See`man upsmon.conf`for details. | |`power.ups.upsmon.monitor.<name>.passwordFile`|`string`| The full path to a file containing the password from`upsd.users`for accessing this UPS. The password file is read on service start. See`upsmon.conf`for details. | |`power.ups.upsmon.monitor.<name>.powerValue`|`signed integer`| Number of power supplies that the UPS feeds on this system. See`upsmon.conf`for details. | |`power.ups.upsmon.monitor.<name>.system`|`string`| Identifier of the UPS to monitor, in this form:`<upsname>\[@<hostname>\[:<port>\]\]`See`upsmon.conf`for details. | |`power.ups.upsmon.monitor.<name>.type`|`string`| The relationship with`upsd`. See `upsmon.conf`for details. | |`power.ups.upsmon.monitor.<name>.user`|`string`| Username from`upsd.users`for accessing this UPS. See`upsmon.conf`for details. | |`power.ups.upsmon.settings`|`attribute set of (atom (null, bool, int, float or string) or list of non-empty (list of (atom (null, bool, int, float or string))))`| Additional settings to add to`upsmon.conf`. | | `power.ups.upsmon.user`|`string`| User to run`upsmon`as.`upsmon.conf`will have its owner set to this user. If not specified, a default user will be created. | |`power.ups.users`|`attribute set of (submodule)`| Users that can access upsd. See`man upsd.users`. | | `power.ups.users.<name>.actions`|`list of string`| Allow the user to do certain things with upsd. See`man upsd.users`for details. | |`power.ups.users.<name>.instcmds`|`list of string`| Let the user initiate specific instant commands. Use "ALL" to grant all commands automatically. For the full list of what your UPS supports, use "upscmd -l". See`man upsd.users`for details. | |`power.ups.users.<name>.passwordFile`|`string`| The full path to a file that contains the user's (clear text) password. The password file is read on service start. | |`power.ups.users.<name>.upsmon`|`null or one of "primary", "secondary"`| Add the necessary actions for a upsmon process to work. See`man upsd.users\` for details. |
